headline: Bernard Arnault's succession, the elephant in the room at LVMH
description: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (6/6). The head of the luxury group deliberately raised his five children so that each one could potentially succeed him. While the family presents a united front, the struggle for succession continues behind the scenes.

headline: Over 100,000 flee or are ordered indoors as wildfires rage in France and Spain
Bab Al-Mandab Strait threatens to be new front in war with Iran
Tariffs: Trump pursues his trade war through every method available
description: The US announced new tariffs on imports from 60 economies, ranging from 10% to 12.5%, on Thursday, as previous measures were set to expire. These rules are set to remain in place for some time, pending possible legal challenges.

headline: A show of strength from Ukrainian society
description: The spontaneous mobilization of Ukrainians, who took to the streets to voice their anger over the dismissal of their defense minister despite relentless Russian strikes, paid off. The country's cohesion ultimately prevailed, while a prolonged crisis would have weakened Ukraine's standing with its allies.

headline: In Brittany, the delicious crêpes at the Kiosque de Mimi
description: 'Gourmet kiosks' (3/6). Across France, small kiosks serving local specialties are a favorite stop for holidaymakers. The Kiosque de Mimi in Vannes, a town in France's Brittany region, offers crêpes at any time of day.

headline: OpenLux: How Luxembourg became China's financial gateway to Europe
description: Beijing has used Luxembourg to invest tens of billions of euros on a massive scale. By acquiring leading technology companies and strategic real estate, it has woven a network that challenges European sovereignty.

How Macron narrowly averted his environment minister's resignation
description: Monique Barbut, who said she intended to quit the government on Tuesday, July 21, after an agricultural law she contested was adopted, ended up staying on in her role on Wednesday. The twist added a colorful note to the political crisis.

headline: Salah Abdeslam, punished for protesting his prison conditions, challenges the administration
description: The last surviving member of the group behind the November 13, 2015, Paris terrorist attacks, Salah Abdeslam was sentenced to life without parole and solitary confinement for 10 years. He has now chosen to confront the authorities.

headline: In the West Bank, a Palestinian hamlet faces relentless harassment by settlers
description: In the Bedouin village of Umm Al-Khair, one of the communities in the southern West Bank surrounded by Israeli settlements and military bases, the Al-Hathaleen family must endure the daily violence of their 'neighbors.'
